name: Naan (Yeastless) India
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Mix well with a spoon or your hands.
2. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and add the eggs. Mix thoroughly.
3. Gradually pour in the milk while continuously stirring until all ingredients are fully incorporated.
4. Transfer the dough to a smooth, non-stick surface like a jelly roll pan or marble slab.
5. Knead the dough for about 10 minutes until it is smooth and forms a soft, slightly sticky ball. Sprinkle with flour occasionally to prevent sticking.
6. Moisten your hands with ghee, shape the dough into a ball, place in a greased bowl, cover with a towel, and let rest in a warm, draft-free area for about 3 hours.
7. Preheat oven and baking sheets to 450°F (232°C).
8. Divide the dough into six equal pieces. Moisten your hands with ghee as needed to shape each piece into a teardrop-shaped leaf, approximately 6 inches long and 3-1/2 inches wide at its widest point, about 3/8 inch thick.
9. Place the bread leaves side by side on the preheated baking sheets and bake for 6 minutes in the middle of the oven until firm to the touch.
10. Broil briefly to lightly brown the tops.
11. Serve hot or at room temperature.
